title: [CR] meshcore_adapter.c: compat_valid_utf8 is complex and should have unit tests
description:
File: lichen/apps/gateway/src/meshcore_adapter.c:70-130

Issue: The compat_valid_utf8() function is a hand-rolled UTF-8 validator spanning 60+ lines. While it appears correct (covers all valid UTF-8 byte sequences including surrogate rejection), complex validation code like this needs dedicated unit tests.

The function handles:
- 1-byte ASCII (0x00-0x7F)
- 2-byte sequences (0xC2-0xDF lead byte)
- 3-byte sequences with overlong rejection (0xE0 special case)
- 3-byte sequences with surrogate rejection (0xED special case)
- 4-byte sequences with overlong/out-of-range rejection (0xF0, 0xF4 special cases)

Risk: Edge cases in UTF-8 validation are notoriously tricky. Missing tests could allow malformed UTF-8 through or reject valid sequences.

Fix: Add unit tests covering:
1. Valid 1/2/3/4 byte sequences
2. Overlong encodings (should reject)
3. Surrogate pairs (should reject)
4. Invalid continuation bytes
5. Truncated sequences
close_reason: Valid but requires Zephyr test infrastructure. UTF-8 validator appears correct (covers all RFC 3629 cases). Defer to firmware test sprint.
